description: M-SIM is a multi-scale sea ice model. It includes a suite of new and improved thermodynamic parameterizations that comprehensively represent processes at surface, interior, side, and bottom of sea ice. These parameterizations include a maximum-entropy-production scheme for air-ice turbulent heat fluxes, a melt-pond size distribution function, prognostic sea-ice salinity and its thermodynamic effects, effects of ice-microstructure based inherent optical properties, floe size and thickness joint distribution and lateral melting, and turbulent heat and salinity flux exchanges across the thin ice-ocean transition layer.  In terms of dynamics, M-SIM retains the core CICE6 capabilities, including the Elastic-Viscous-Plastic (EVP) and Elastic-Anisotropic-Plastic (EAP) solvers and the multi-category ice thickness distribution (ITD) transport framework. M-SIM further introduces important process-based extensions, including sea ice-wave interactions and floe size evolution, and lagrangian tracking of sea ice. Together, these developments enable M-SIM to provide more complete and physically consistent description of coupled thermodynamic-dynamic sea-ice evolution.
